title: iris chamaeiris subsp. balkana
intro: Iris chamaeiris subsp. balkana, also known as Balkan iris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Iris, native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating skin conditions and inflammation, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Balkan iris include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce swelling and pain, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and roots can be used to create infusions, tinctures, or ointments, often used topically to treat various skin issues and infections.
